title: Chewy Stock (CHWY): Here's What Investor Sentiment Signals
text: Individual investors have a very positive view of Chewy, with the number of portfolios holding the stock increasing by 5% in the last 30 days. The recent investment in Chewy by retail trader Keith Gill, also known as Roaring Kitty, has propelled the stock upward. Chewy has seen a 20.4% decline in the past month amid mixed market sentiment and cautious analyst views. However, the stock has surged about 24% over the past six months, driven by Chewy's robust financial performance, its expansion into the pet pharmacy sector, and various other diversification efforts. Based on analysis of 43 Wall Street analysts, Chewy has a median price target of $41.00, with the highest price target at $50.00 and the lowest at $28.00. According to current analyst ratings, CHWY has 20 Buy ratings, 5 Hold ratings, and 0 Sell ratings. Wall Street analysts predict CHWY stock could reach $41.00 in the next 12 months, representing an 89.4% increase. While the popular narrative sees Chewy as undervalued with a fair value of $40.77 per share, the current P/E of 40.5x indicates some valuation concerns despite bullish sentiment from many analysts. The 23 analysts covering Chewy stock have a consensus rating of Strong Buy and an average price target of $43.39, forecasting an 88.69% increase over the next year.
